notes: No more black textures using Wine tkg-re4-4.4 downloaded from Lutris. Had to install the directx9 provided from the game files into the Wineprefix to not crash at startup. Game runs perfectly on 30 FPS mode in the options but with 60 fps the games runs in slow motion because the in game time is calculated per frame rendered, not per second passed IRL and (without using 100% cpu nor gpu) my computer can't get 60 fps stable performance. There's clearly a bottleneck somewhere in the code of Wine in dx9 to OpenGL translation but I'm not aware of a fix by now in this game. The best option would be waiting for projects like D9VK to work. No frames gained with Esync on or off but slightly more stable frametimes with it disabled. Since this is a experimental patch, it might take a long time to be implemented into future Proton versions. I had to run Windows Steam under this Wine version to start the game without Proton and with the re4 fix patch. Sidenote: the wine executable binary file you download from Lutris will be stored into ~/.local/share/lutris/runners/wine/(version)/bin/wine

title: resident evil 4 / biohazard 4

notes:
Fixed lighting problem by using d9vk
 instructions:
 1 - download latest release: https://github.com/Joshua-Ashton/d9vk/releases
2 - extract and open terminal inside extracted folder
3 - On terminal run: "export WINEPREFIX= your_steam_folder_here/steamapps/compatdata/254700/pfx/
./setup_dxvk.sh install" (without " ")            

After that, the game ran as expected
title: resident evil 4 / biohazard 4

notes:
It works nearly-perfectly, but the biggest requirement is using D9VK (https://github.com/Joshua-Ashton/d9vk/releases). This allows Proton (Wine) to correctly render the lighting in this case. No other changes are needed. Here is how you install it:       (1)-Download the latest release from the page above, under the Assets section for the latest post. (2)-Extract the archive in any given folder, or just your Downloads folder. Go into the newly-extracted folder and open a Terminal. (3)-Find your Steam install folder, then type this into the Terminal: "export WINEPREFIX=(Your Steam Folder)/steamapps/compatdata/254700/pfx/" followed by "./setup_dxvk.sh install"      A Wine dialog box should pop up telling you that it's updating the configuration. If any popups ask you to install something, then hit Cancel to not install that component, as you should have what you need for the game already. After a minute or two, it should finish, and you are ready to play.     Now, if you get an error after the script command that says something like "invalid wine-prefix", then the path that you entered is most likely incorrect. Double-check it, but note that you are required to enter the full path, including your Home folder. For example, my path was "/home/<user>/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/..." That should resolve the issue.

     Shout-out to the guy who reviewed RE4 a day or two ago for listing the trick, I just tried to clarify and expand.
title: resident evil 4 / biohazard 4

notes: It works now with D9VK! Either install it yourself manually into your Resident Evil 4 Proton prefix (https://github.com/Joshua-Ashton/d9vk/) or use the version that comes with Proton 4.11+ by: Right Click on Resident Evil 4 -> Properties -> Set Launch Options -> PROTON_USE_D9VK=1 %command% - This will give a full 60fps. I only encountered some very light stutter and it was only maybe two or three times. Pretty much a native experience!

title: resident evil 4 / biohazard 4

notes: Set "PROTON_USE_D9VK=1 %command" for flawless performance and no graphical issues. Fixes the black texture and stuttering of the game. Also, my Steam Controller now works fine with the game. No noticeable issues, no crashes experienced so far, runs basically like native.
title: resident evil 4 / biohazard 4

notes:
Enabling D9VK did fix the graphical and slowdown issues for me, as it has done for other people reporting here.
However, the game would freeze, seemingly at random, after some playtime, necessitating killing the game process. This rendered the game essentially unplayable when it became impossible to reach the next save point (in Chapter 1-3) before the game would freeze. 

The solution for me was to also disable esync in addition to enabling D9VK, using the launch options "PROTON_USE_D9VK=1 PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command%".

It might be worth noting that I have Resident Evil 4 and Proton 4.11 installed on an NTFS disk separate from the Steam installation itself on Linux on an ext4 disk (I followed this guide: https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/wiki/Using-a-NTFS-disk-with-Linux-and-Windows). Making NTFS disks work in conjunction with Proton can be a challenge in itself and I am not sure if my problem was related to this fact or not.
title: resident evil 4 / biohazard 4

notes: So happy to see this work in Proton! Like everyone mentioned, using D9vk is a must. I found it best to set antialiasing to x4, after that it runs almost flawlessly smooth. There's still some choppiness around aiming and firing, just holding down left click while panning the camera around causes the game to stutter. This does not happen with a steam controller using the mouse input. The biggest issue with mouse and keyboard is you can not shoot and aim at the same time, which ruins the fun of the chicago typewriter. Regardless, this is super playable, even in the mercenaries game mode.
